The identity (GUID) of the eDiscovery case that was created, changed, or deleted.
The name of the eDiscovery case that was created, changed, or deleted.
eDiscovery cmdlet activities have a value of EMC for this property. This indicates the activity was performed by using the Microsoft Purview portal GUI or running the cmdlet in PowerShell.
The IP address of the device that was used when the activity was logged. The IP address is displayed in either an IPv4 or IPv6 address format.
For eDiscovery activities, this property is typically blank.
The build number for the version of the Microsoft Purview portal running in your organization.
The date and time in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) when the eDiscovery activity was completed.
A list of source ID, source name, and location details associated to the activity.
The name of the Microsoft  365 organization.

Additional properties related to the eDiscovery activity. For example, when case members are updated, this field contains the updated case member information; or when the review set description is updated, this field contains the updated review set description updated by the user.
The ID of the report entry. The ID uniquely identifies the audit log entry.
The name of the item associated with the activity. For example, this field would contain the name of the document viewed when user views a review set document.
The GUID of the eDiscovery process.
The GUID of the object (for example, a search, hold, or review set) that was created, accessed, or changed, by the activity listed in the Operation property.
The name of the object (for example, a search, hold or review set) that was created, accessed, or changed, by the activity listed in the Operation property.
The type of eDiscovery object that the user created, deleted, or modified. For example, a search action (generate sample results or trigger an export from search) are listed as Search in this field.
The name of the operation that corresponds to the eDiscovery activity that was performed.
The GUID for your Microsoft 365 organization.
The name of the input file used to generate the query. This is specific to the search by file gesture.
The GUID of the query associated with the activity.
The query text associated with the activity, such as a search statistic process or add to review process.
The type of operation indicated by the record.
Indicates whether the action (specified in the Operation property) was successful or not.
Settings applied to the eDiscovery activity.
The date and time in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) when the eDiscovery activity was started.
Whether the specific activity was canceled by the user.
The user who performed the activity (specified in the Operation property) that resulted in the record being logged.
An alternative ID for the user identified in the UserId property. For eDiscovery activities, the value for this property is typically the same as the UserId property.
The  subscription used by your organization. For eDiscovery activities, this property is typically blank.

The type of user that performed the operation. The following values indicate the user type.   0   A regular user. 2   An administrator in your organization. 3   A Microsoft datacenter administrator or datacenter system account. 4   A system account. 5   An application. 6   A service principal.
Indicates the version number of the activity (identified by the Operation property) that's logged.
The service where the activity occurred.
